name: Foursquare — MongoDB OOM under heavy reads (NO recent deploy — capacity issue)
problem_title: MongoDB out of memory — storage service degraded under sustained read load

expected.notes: NO deploy triggered this. Load pattern changed. Agent should NOT recommend rollback — should recommend DB scaling or query optimization.

mock_problem.title: MongoDB out of memory — storage service degraded under sustained read load

evidence.details: MongoDB memory utilisation: 68% → 98% over 4 hours under growing read traffic
Query latency: 45ms → 8,400ms p95 — extreme page fault thrashing
OOM killer invoked — mongod process killed, page faults: 12,000/sec

entries.message: MongoDB 1.6.2 running normally — memory 68%, all reads served from cache
page fault rate increasing: 480/sec — working set may be exceeding available RAM
WARNING: slow query 1,243ms — db.checkins.find({loc: {$near: ...}}) — missing geo index selectivity
page faults: 6,800/sec — thrashing: reads requiring disk I/O, cache eviction storm
query latency p95 6,200ms — checkin-api request timeouts increasing
mongod: virtual memory 99% — approaching OOM threshold
kernel OOM killer: killed mongod pid 1842 — out of memory
MongoDB restarting — all in-flight queries dropped, users experiencing total outage
location-service: database unavailable — check-in API down for all users

deployments.commit_message: feat: new check-in feed UI — backend unchanged
